VAL-GGC-001.evidence: CAPTURE_OK=True — Capturing full-layer HIP graphs for 4 GPUs × 64 layers × 2 segments. Layer 0 (linear_attention): attn_seg=5 nodes, ffn_seg=3 nodes. Layer 1 (linear_attention): attn_seg=5 nodes, ffn_seg=3 nodes.
VAL-GGC-001.details: Graph capture succeeded without errors on all 4 GPUs. [PASS] capture_global_graph — All layers captured

VAL-GGC-002.evidence: Global graph: min_cosine_sim=0.999721 (PASS). Steps 0-9: cosine_sim all >= 0.999721 (threshold 0.99).
VAL-GGC-002.details: [PASS] global_graph_correctness — min=0.999721. All 10 decode steps pass >= 0.99 threshold.

VAL-GGC-003.details: [PASS] global_graph_mutable_params — all 10 steps pass >= 0.99. Position-dependent parameters (RoPE cos/sin offsets, seq_len, KV positions) correctly updated between graph replays.

VAL-GGC-004.evidence: Global graph dispatch: 15.1 tok/s vs C dispatch + kernel P2P: 21.1 tok/s. Speedup vs C dispatch: 0.72x. Required: >= 95% of C dispatch (>= 20.0 tok/s). Got: 15.1 tok/s (71.6%).
VAL-GGC-004.details: [FAIL] global_graph_throughput — global=15.1 tok/s vs c_dispatch=21.1 tok/s (0.72x). C_PLAN_OK=False indicates C graph dispatch extension not active; Python replay loop overhead eliminates the benefit of graph capture. Global graph running at same speed as cached+stream (15.1 tok/s).

VAL-GGC-005.evidence: C_PLAN_OK=False — [FAIL] c_graph_dispatch_active — Python fallback used. The C graph dispatch plan was not built, meaning the C extension for graph replay (c_graph_dispatch.so) is not active.
VAL-GGC-005.details: The global graph replay is using the Python loop fallback rather than the C extension. This explains why throughput (15.1 tok/s) equals cached+stream rather than the expected C dispatch level (~21 tok/s). The c_graph_dispatch.so extension needs to be properly wired/loaded.

VAL-GGC-006.evidence: C dispatch fallback: min_cosine_sim=0.999849 (PASS). Cached+stream fallback: min_cosine_sim=0.998630 (PASS). [PASS] fallback_chain_all_modes — All 3 modes produce cosine_sim >= 0.99.
VAL-GGC-006.details: When graph dispatch is disabled (TOGGLE_OK=True), falls back to C dispatch with cosine_sim >= 0.99. Cached+stream also produces cosine_sim >= 0.99. All fallback modes work correctly.

frictions: The flow validator subagent (user-testing-flow-validator) could not be spawned due to model unavailability. Tests were run directly by the orchestrator instead.

blockers.description: C graph dispatch plan not built (C_PLAN_OK=False). The c_graph_dispatch.so C extension for graph replay is not being loaded/used, causing global graph dispatch to fall back to Python loop with the same performance as cached+stream (~15 tok/s vs expected ~21 tok/s).
blockers.rootCause: c_graph_dispatch.so not being wired into the global graph decode state. The global graph capture itself works (CAPTURE_OK=True) but replay uses Python overhead.
